findings.issue: Task 02 (step 4, AC, Details) instructs adding the backendkiller import 'in alphabetical position between failconns and forkconns'. That is alphabetically wrong: the cmd/app.go import block is alphabetical (deadlocks, failconns, forkconns, idlexacts, log, rollbacks, tempfiles, terminate, waitxacts), and 'backendkiller' sorts BEFORE 'deadlocks', so it must be the FIRST workload import. Following the hint literally produces a non-alphabetical import block. Note the internal inconsistency: Task 03 correctly states backendkiller sorts first (before deadlocks) for the README impact table, while Task 02 places it mid-list.
findings.fix: Insert "github.com/lesovsky/noisia/backendkiller" as the first workload import in cmd/app.go (before deadlocks, after the package's own non-noisia imports if any), not between failconns and forkconns. Align Task 02 wording with Task 03's correct alphabetization.

findings.issue: Task 02 says to place the launch if-block 'after the forkconns block (before wg.Wait())' and the helper 'after startForkconnsWorkload'. This is fine for the launch block and helper (those lists are insertion-order, not alphabetical, in cmd/app.go), so no conflict there — flagged only to contrast with the import-ordering issue above so the implementer does not over-correct and also reorder the launch blocks/helpers.
findings.fix: Keep launch if-block and start helper in insertion order (after forkconns) as the task says; only the IMPORT line needs alphabetical-first placement.

verified.tdd_adequacy_task01: Adequate. TDD Anchor covers the three AC clusters: TestConfig_validate (table: Rate=0 valid, Rate<0 invalid, PlanSize<1, ReportInterval<=0, empty Conninfo), Test_buildPrepare (builder unit: exact target-list width + unique name, no bind args - covers the no-injection AC), TestWorkload_Run (testcontainers: monotonic counter growth + clean nil on ctx timeout + invalid-conninfo error). Mechanics-only (no real OOM) is correct for CID. Pattern matches rollbacks_test.go (assert.Positive on loop-helper). Test paths follow project convention (in-package _test.go + one-line main_test.go via dbtest.RunMain, -p 1).
